title: Spiranthera peltata
taxonomy: Spiranthera peltata is classified within the family Rutaceae, which includes citrus and other flowering plants. It belongs to the genus Spiranthera, a group of flowering plants native to South America. The species S. peltata is further classified as a species within the genus, indicating its unique characteristics. Within the Rutaceae family, S. peltata is part of the subfamily Aurantioideae, which contains various fruit-bearing plants. This classification suggests a close relationship with other Rutaceae species in terms of morphology and evolution.

intro: Spiranthera peltata is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the "Guatemalan orris root". This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Spiranthera peltata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Spiranthera peltata is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various herbal remedies and supplements.
